attacking_style: Operates from long-range breakaways, showing capacity to sustain efforts on extended stages and finish strongly when the break stays clear. Demonstrates finishing speed to contest reduced sprint finishes from breaks.
team_tactics: Limited data on team setup. Appears to be given freedom for opportunistic breakaway attempts rather than being a protected leader.
terrain_preference: Long, attrition-based stages that favor endurance over pure climbing or sprinting. Best suited to breakaway scenarios on stages where peloton may miscalculate or fatigue.
vulnerability: Unlikely to succeed when peloton controls tempo tightly or when pure climbers/sprinters are motivated. Depends on peloton tactical errors or fatigue rather than overpowering opponents.
key_patterns: Breakaway specialist: both recorded results came from long-range break attempts (2 observations)
Strong finale from breaks: podium finish capability when break succeeds, 1st and 3rd recorded
Endurance-focused rider: success on longest Paris-Nice stage highlights stamina over explosive power
form_trajectory: positive: progression from breakaway podium at 2019 Vuelta to first pro win at 2022 Paris-Nice shows development
